title: Bridge Paper: Perspectives on Rehabilitation Using Non-invasive Brain Stimulation Based on Second-Person Neuroscience of Teaching-Learning Interactions.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Social Incarnation** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Intersubjectivity — second-person neuroscience, embodied empathy, shared intentionality, social predictive processing.
**Phase context:** intersubjectivity, embodied empathy, predictive processing social cognition, shared intentionality, second-person neuroscience

Paper: Perspectives on Rehabilitation Using Non-invasive Brain Stimulation Based on Second-Person Neuroscience of Teaching-Learning Interactions.
DOI: 10.3389/fpsyg.2021.789637
Abstract: Recent advances in second-person neuroscience have allowed the underlying neural mechanisms involved in teaching-learning interactions to be better understood. Teaching is not merely a one-way transfer of information from teacher to student; it is a complex interaction that requires metacognitive and mentalizing skills to understand others' intentions and integrate information regarding oneself and others.
